findings.title: Critical vulnerability in base image OpenSSL
findings.description: The base image mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:6.0 contains OpenSSL 1.1.1k which is vulnerable to CVE-2024-0727 (CVSS 9.8). This allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted certificate chains.
findings.fixedIn: openssl 3.0.13 or use updated base image
findings.remediation: Update to a patched base image version such as mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:6.0.28-jammy or migrate to .NET 8.0

findings.title: Database connection string with embedded credentials
findings.description: Hardcoded database credentials detected in application configuration. The connection string contains plaintext username and password which could be extracted from the container image.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials from configuration files. Use environment variables, Azure Key Vault, or other secret management solutions to inject credentials at runtime.

findings.title: Azure AD client secret exposed
findings.description: Azure Active Directory client secret found in configuration file. This credential could allow unauthorized access to Azure resources.
findings.remediation: Remove client secret from configuration. Use Managed Identity or inject via environment variable 'AzureAd__ClientSecret'.

findings.title: Denial of Service vulnerability in System.Text.Json
findings.description: System.Text.Json version 6.0.0 is vulnerable to CVE-2024-30105 which allows attackers to cause denial of service via deeply nested JSON objects.
findings.remediation: Update System.Text.Json to version 6.0.10 or higher. Note: Ensure compatibility with Azure.Identity which has a transitive dependency on System.Text.Json.

findings.title: Container running as root user
findings.description: The container is configured to run as the root user (UID 0). This violates the principle of least privilege and increases the attack surface.
findings.remediation: Add 'USER app' directive to run as non-root user. The .NET base images include a pre-created 'app' user for this purpose.

findings.title: Image size exceeds recommended limit
findings.description: The final image size is 847MB which exceeds the recommended limit of 500MB. Large images increase attack surface and deployment time.
findings.remediation: Consider using Alpine-based images or multi-stage builds to reduce image size.

findings.title: Redis password in configuration
findings.description: Redis connection string contains embedded password.
findings.remediation: Inject Redis credentials via environment variable.

findings.title: Third-party API key detected
findings.description: API key for external service found in configuration.
findings.remediation: Use secret management solution for API keys.

findings.title: Newtonsoft.Json deprecated API usage
findings.description: The application uses Newtonsoft.Json which has deprecated serialization APIs. Consider migrating to System.Text.Json.
findings.remediation: This is informational only. Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.1 has no known security vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Low severity vulnerability in zlib
findings.description: zlib 1.2.11 has a low severity buffer overflow in inflate (CVE-2022-37434).
